demographics.living_situation: lives at home with both parents

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Parent: "He has had a burning fever for six days that Panadol won't touch, his eyes are bloodshot, and his lips are bleeding. Today he's gone really floppy and an hour ago he suddenly cried out and grabbed at his chest."
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The fever won't come down after six days of alternating Panadol and Nurofen, and the parents are frightened by how quickly he is going downhill — especially the sudden chest episode today.

symptom_narrative.onset: the fever started six days ago and has not broken
symptom_narrative.character: a burning, high fever that keeps coming back within a couple of hours of medicine; on top of it the parents have watched his eyes, lips, skin, hands and feet change over the last day or two
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst he has ever been — inconsolable, floppy and not himself at all
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: whole body — fever, red eyes, cracked lips, a rash on his tummy, and red puffy sore hands and feet
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: fever is almost constant and barely dips with medicine; he became lethargic and irritable today
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing — Panadol and Nurofen only take the edge off for an hour or two
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps going regardless of what they give him
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: bloodshot eyes with no goop or discharge, bright red cracked and bleeding lips, a very red bumpy tongue, a red blotchy rash on his trunk, red swollen sore hands and feet so he refuses to walk, and a tender lump on the side of his neck; an hour ago he suddenly cried and clutched at his chest
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: never been this sick before; normally a well, fully vaccinated 4-year-old
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: lethargic, refusing to walk or eat, inconsolable and irritable

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: normally a healthy child, no ongoing medical problems

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with both parents who are present and making the call; parents are exhausted and highly distressed by his continuous decline

objective_data_offered.value: measured at home around 39-39.5 C with an ear thermometer, not settling with Panadol or Nurofen
objective_data_offered.timing: repeatedly over the last 6 days, most recently this morning
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: home ear thermometer, single readings by an anxious parent

objective_data_offered.value: both eyes are bloodshot / red with no pus or sticky discharge
lips are bright red, cracked and bleeding slightly; his tongue looks very red and bumpy
a red blotchy rash spread over his tummy and body
his hands and feet look red and puffy/swollen and he won't put weight on them
a tender lump about 2 cm on one side of his neck
he is floppy, lethargic, irritable and inconsolable today; an hour ago he suddenly cried out and grabbed at his chest
numerous empty paracetamol and ibuprofen bottles around the house
